title: Supreme Author
description:
Is the Genome conscious? If it were conscious, what would it say? 

In this drawing, the hand represents the Genome (which is why it has an ATGC on it). The pen represents the mechanisms of transcription, translation, ontogeny, and phylogeny -- in short, evolution. The line of text says, "help! I am trapped in the genome!", and can either be interpreted as either an mRNA transcript, or as the organism's psychophysical phenotype. 

As I drew this piece, my thought process: 
1. If the genome were conscious, it would want to demonstrate its consciousness to us. 
2. Since we don't observe the genome communicating with us obviously, let us postulate that the genome is not conscious. 
3. Who is the "I" who is making this drawing? If the "I" exists at the organismal level, then this drawing is just a human aesthetic & esoteric experience. But if the "I" is in the genome, then I have falsified that the genome is not conscious (statement 2). Perhaps we can demonstrate the consciousness of the genome via proof-by-contradiction. 
4. So then really, before I can ask "who" the Self is, I need to ask "what" the Self is. Whether the Self is essential or relational, designed or autopoetic, timeless or transient, local or universal, I do not even know the answer to these basic questions. 
5. I do not know what the Self is. I am hungry to know what the Self is. How will I find out what the Self is? 
6. I ("my I" or "an I" or "the I"?) cannot enter into the Unknown carrying a map of the Known. To "explore" while holding a map of the Known is to disregard the Unknown, and to actively deny the possibility of the Unknowable. 
7. Stillness emerges, engages, expands, envelops. 

AUM

~~~~~~~~
~~~~~~~~
~~~~~~~~

“The ability to observe without evaluating is the highest form of intelligence.”
― Jiddu Krishnamurti

“Yesterday I was clever, so I wanted to change the world. Today I am wise, so I am changing myself.”
― Rumi

“His life oscillates, as everyone's does, not merely between two poles, such as the body and the spirit, the saint and the sinner, but between thousands and thousands.”
― Hermann Hesse, Steppenwolf 

"We may need [our personality] when we're young, but it becomes a habit. The fear is that we lose our way if we dispose of our personality. Actually we only lose a crutch. We aren't free until then"
―Keith Jarrett

"I do my thing and you do your thing. I am not in this world to live up to your expectations, And you are not in this world to live up to mine. You are you, and I am I, and if by chance we find each other, it's beautiful. If not, it can't be helped."
― Fritz Perls, "Gestalt Therapy Verbatim", 1969

"Errare humanum est, perseverare autem diabolicum, et tertia non datur."
[To err is human; to persevere in error is of the devil; and no third possibility is granted.]
― Seneca
